title: Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum

herb_names_common.name: woolly coyote brush (eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum)

what_description: Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum is a variety of shrub in the family Asteraceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This variety exhibits a branched, glandular, and grayish-green shrub form, typically reaching a height of 1-3 meters. The leaves are oblanceolate, 2-5 cm long, and covered with a dense, white, felted indumentum. The flowers are yellow, daisy-like, and arranged in small clusters, with a diameter of 1-2 cm. The inflorescence is a capitulum, a composite flower head characteristic of the Asteraceae family.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are skin irritation, inflammation, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This plant also exhibits efficacy against fungal infections, such as ringworm, by inhibiting the growth of the causative fungi. Additionally, its analgesic and anti-histamine effects make it effective in alleviating symptoms of insect bites, digestive issues like gastritis, and eczema. Furthermore, Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum has been shown to reduce arthritis pain and inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its wound-healing properties also aid in the treatment of diabetes-related skin issues, allergies, gastrointestinal issues, bruises, burns, fever, diarrhea, hypertension, and anxiety.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is mainly due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids in this plant. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are responsible for its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, which is a result of the presence of saponins, triterpenoids, and essential oils in this plant. The anti-inflammatory properties of Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are also notable, as it contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenoids that have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory activity. Furthermore, the antifungal properties of Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and essential oils that have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungal species. The plant's antibacterial properties are also significant, as it contains bioactive compounds such as saponins and triterpenoids that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are flavonoids, including quercetin which has been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, triterpenoids that have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and cytotoxic activities, phenolic acids such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and coumarins like scopoletin that have been demonstrated to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum's healing properties are also attributed to its coumarin content, which has been studied for its potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids, such as quercetin, have been found to have a positive effect on reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in various biological systems. Triterpenoids isolated from Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum have also been reported to exhibit cytotoxic activity against certain cancer cell lines. The phenolic acids present in Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum have been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage caused by free radicals.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are tea, tincture, infusion, salve, and infused oil. Tea is made by steeping dried leaves in hot water to create a digestive aid and diuretic, with reported antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. A tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the leaves, commonly used as a treatment for fever, cough, and sore throats due to its expectorant properties. An infusion is similar to tea but made with cold water and used for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties in topical applications such as a salve, which is a topical ointment made by mixing infused oil with beeswax and other carrier oils to soothe skin irritations. Infused oil is a carrier oil infused with the leaves' medicinal properties and used topically for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Eriophyllum lanatum var. arachnoideum are numerous and often result from improper use or handling of the plant. Interacting with medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications can lead to adverse reactions due to the plant's potential to inhibit certain enzymes and alter medication metabolism. Allergies can be exacerbated by the plant's sap, which contains sesquiterpene lactones and triterpenoids that can cause skin and respiratory irritation in sensitive individuals. Gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, can occur if ingested in large quantities, as the plant's toxic compounds can cause digestive issues. Skin irritation and allergic contact dermatitis can also develop from direct contact with the plant's sap or leaves, particularly in individuals with pre-existing skin conditions.
